title: hieracium coadunatum
intro: Hieracium coadunatum, also known as Rough Hawk's Beard or Rough Hawkbit, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion-like Asteraceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Hieracium coadunatum include reducing swelling and pain, as well as aiding in wound healing and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Hieracium coadunatum include infusions and decoctions, often used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and skin irritations.
